Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Coding My Handwriting 54 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. An investigation of the forces behind the age-verification bills 99 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 1001 upvotes, 263 comments
  4. OpenTelemetry for Rust Developers 40 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. The Wyden Siren Goes Off Again: We'll Be "Stunned" by NSA Under Section 702 395 upvotes, 119 comments
  6. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 350 upvotes, 106 comments
  7. I Found 39 Algolia Admin Keys Exposed Across Open Source Documentation Sites 92 upvotes, 22 comments
  8. Kovan: From Production MVCC Systems to Wait-Free Memory Reclamation 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Using Thunderbird for RSS 77 upvotes, 18 comments
  10. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 430 upvotes, 139 comments
  11. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 223 upvotes, 70 comments
  12. Lost Doctor Who episodes found 238 upvotes, 76 comments
  13. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 66 upvotes, 16 comments
  14. Shipping Grayscale Photos at Small Scale 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Hammerspoon 230 upvotes, 84 comments
  16. Our Experience with I-Ready 25 upvotes, 6 comments
  17. Optimizing Content for Agents 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  18. New 'negative light' technology hides data transfers in plain sight 71 upvotes, 44 comments
  19. Hyperlinks in terminal emulators 86 upvotes, 58 comments
  20. Launch HN: Captain (YC W26) – Automated RAG for Files 45 upvotes, 28 comments
  21. Stanford researchers report first recording of a blue whale's heart rate (2019) 57 upvotes, 38 comments
  22. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 64 upvotes, 45 comments
  23. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 88 upvotes, 66 comments
  24. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 463 upvotes, 424 comments
  25. Your phone is an entire computer 264 upvotes, 249 comments
  26. Source code of Swedish e-government services has been leaked 208 upvotes, 205 comments
  27. Show HN: Robotics runtime in the browser (flight controller, WebAssembly) 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. John Carmack about open source and anti-AI activists 255 upvotes, 350 comments
  29. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 210 upvotes, 295 comments
  30. Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters 352 upvotes, 532 comments

Last generated 11:30am, 14 March 2026. Source here.
